name: German Lamb in Sour Cream
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Lean, boneless lamb (cut into cubes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the lamb in oil in a skillet, then drain well.
2. In a bowl, combine 1/2 cup flour with salt, dill seed, caraway seed, and rosemary. Toss the lamb cubes to coat thoroughly.
3. Place the coated lamb cubes in a lightly oiled slow cooker. Stir in beef broth, vegetable oil, tarragon vinegar, remaining ingredients (except second measure of flour, water, and sour cream).
4. Cover and cook on LOW for 10-14 hours.
5. Turn heat to HIGH for 30 minutes before serving.
6. In a separate bowl, combine second measure of flour with water. Stir into the slow cooker until thickened.
7. Add sour cream and stir again.
8. Serve over hot buttered noodles and garnish with additional sour cream.
